# BAA Playbook Matrix

Reference table from the Trinetix Contract Playbook. Use these as the deterministic clause-by-clause ceiling alongside the per-type SKILL.md.

| ID / Topic | Preferred position | Acceptable fallback | Red flags | Escalation | AI instruction |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BAA-001 When BAA is needed | Use only where Trinetix receives, creates, maintains, or transmits PHI as subcontractor to a business associate. | If no PHI, use privacy/security terms instead. | BAA signed “just in case” with no PHI scope or service need. | Privacy/CLCO review. | Ask whether PHI is actually involved and describe PHI flow. |
| BAA-002 Purpose limitation | Use/disclose PHI only as necessary to perform services under underlying agreement. | Add project-specific permitted uses if needed. | Broad PHI use; marketing/data analytics/de-identification rights without approval. | Privacy review. | Check permitted PHI uses against service scope. |
| BAA-003 Safeguards | Administrative, physical, and technical safeguards; written information security program; audit evidence within 10 days on request. | Provide SOC/ISO/security summary if full audit evidence is too broad. | Onsite audits anytime; unrestricted access to systems; audit evidence beyond HIPAA scope. | Security/Privacy review. | Flag audit/evidence requests and feasibility. |
| BAA-004 Subcontractors | No delegation without prior written consent; PHI recipients must agree to same restrictions/conditions. | General subprocessors pre-approved list may be negotiated. | No subcontractors/affiliates at all where delivery needs them; no practical consent process. | Delivery/Privacy review. | Check if affiliates/contractors can lawfully support services. |
| BAA-005 Breach notice | Notify within 2 business days of suspected/actual breach/security incident/unauthorized use or disclosure; include known affected individuals and details. | Accept “without undue delay, no later than X days” only if business and security can comply. | Immediate notice for any attempted incident; unrealistic investigation detail before known. | Security/Privacy review. | Classify incident notice threshold and deadline. |
| BAA-006 Minimum necessary | Request/disclose only minimum PHI necessary. | No fallback needed. | Client pushes bulk PHI access not required for services. | Privacy review. | Flag overbroad PHI access. |
| BAA-007 Return/destruction | Return or destroy PHI at termination; if infeasible, protections continue and use limited to infeasibility purpose. | Add backup/archive exception if needed. | Absolute deletion from backups on impossible timeline; no infeasibility exception. | Privacy/IT review. | Check deletion obligations for technical feasibility. |
| BAA-008 Indemnity and breach costs | Template includes broad indemnity and reimbursement of breach notification/credit monitoring costs. | Keep tied to breach caused by Subcontractor and subject to liability section where possible. | Uncapped indemnity despite BAA liability cap; fines not caused by Trinetix; client negligence included. | CLCO review. | Check relationship between indemnity, breach costs, and liability cap. |
| BAA-009 Insurance | Cyber coverage: $1.5M per claim/incident, $3M aggregate; includes network/privacy liability, first-party expense, business interruption, extortion; additional insured on request. | Align with actual policies. | Coverage requirements unavailable under policy; tail coverage not commercially available; client requires higher limits without deal value. | Finance/Risk review. | Compare insurance obligations against actual certificates. |
| BAA-010 Liability cap | Absent gross negligence/willful misconduct, liability capped at greater of required insurance proceeds or $3M for insured-type loss. | Accept SOW-specific cap only with CLCO approval. | Unlimited PHI liability; cap removed; cap lower than insurance obligation unintentionally; no carve-out alignment. | CLCO approval. | Extract cap, exceptions, and interaction with underlying agreement. |
| BAA-011 Third-party beneficiaries | Clients of Business Associate are third-party beneficiaries with enforcement/indemnification rights. | Accept only if downstream client identities and PHI scope are known. | Unknown unlimited third-party beneficiary class; direct claims by many clients without cap/process. | CLCO review. | Flag third-party beneficiary rights and affected parties. |
| BAA-012 Drafting cleanup | Fix blank party fields, notice emails, duplicated punctuation, and defined terms before use. | N/A | Unsigned blanks or missing notice recipients. | Legal before signature. | Check all placeholders and notice emails. |
